title: ADR 0001 — Placements funnel conversion capability
content: Built typed pure function computeFunnelConversion() in src/features/placements/lib/conversion.ts computing stage-to-stage (stepPct) and overall sourced→hired (overallPct) conversion, all clamped to [0,100] via one safePct() helper (no NaN/Infinity/overflow). Wired into FunnelCard via formatPercent. Established repo conventions: feature-specific logic in src/features//lib (src/lib = cross-cutting only); test runner is Node built-in node:test (npm test), zero deps, relying on Node 24 native TS; tsconfig allowImportingTsExtensions enabled with .ts-extension imports reserved for test files. Open follow-up: clamping hides dirty data (later stage > earlier) — future ADR may surface a data-quality warning. Verified: 6/6 tests pass, tsc clean.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 10:10 UTC
ADR 0001 placements funnel conversion — APPROVED
content: Reviewed and APPROVED the funnel conversion capability in src/features/placements/lib/conversion.ts (computeFunnelConversion + safePct). Independently verified: tsc --noEmit exit 0, npm test 6/6 pass, eslint clean; all handoff-claimed files confirmed present on disk. No security/correctness gating issues — the safePct helper centralizes division and guarantees finite, [0,100]-clamped output. Accepted (do NOT re-flag): the documented >100% clamp that hides dirty data is a deliberate trade-off with a deferred follow-up ADR. Open low-severity notes for future runs: (1) FunnelCard.tsx opacity '1-index*0.12' is unbounded for >=9 stages; (2) package.json test glob needs Node>=21 engines declaration to avoid false-green zero-test runs; (3) safePct lacks a numerator finiteness guard.

M2 [pii] src/features/placements/data.ts:28-55 hardcoded candidate names + background-check details (DUI 2017 L30, clean MVR L28, MVR review L44) — OPEN. L1 [dependency] package.json:13 @google/generative-ai declared but unused in src and deprecated — OPEN. Verified clean: no secrets in src (.env.example placeholders empty, .env/.pem/.gcp gitignored), no eval/exec/injection/innerHTML/raw-SQL/network — app is static/presentational with no auth or I/O surface. npm audit works in this repo (ran clean: 2 moderate, 0 high/critical).

## Security Scan Report — 2026-05-27

**Codebase:** Apprenticeship Placement (`C:/Users/mkdol/xprice/apprenticeship-placement`) · Next.js 16 / React 19 / TypeScript
**Coverage:** ✅ Full — dependency audit (`npm audit` ran cleanly), secret detection (pattern sweep), code-pattern analysis (eval/exec/SQL/innerHTML/network/PII). No scan phase failed.

**Severity counts:** Critical 0 · High 0 · Medium 2 · Low 1

```chart
Critical: 0
High: 0
Medium: 2
Low: 1
```

### 🟠 Medium

**M1 — Known CVE: PostCSS XSS via unescaped `` (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93, CVSS 6.1)**
`node_modules/next/node_modules/postcss` @ **8.4.31** (transitive via `next`) — confirmed `package-lock.json`.
Risk: PostCSS `<8.5.10` can emit unescaped `` in stringify output → XSS (CWE-79). Build-time CSS tool, so real exposure here is low (no attacker-controlled CSS pipeline today), but it's a known advisory — ranked Medium per "known CVE regardless of reachability."
Fix: bump `next` to a release that bundles `postcss >=8.5.10`, or add a `package.json` `overrides`: `"overrides": { "postcss": ">=8.5.10" }` then `npm install`. Re-run `npm audit` to confirm 0.

**M2 — Sensitive candidate background data committed to repo**
`src/features/placements/data.ts:28-55` — candidate names paired with background-check details: `"DUI 2017"` (line 30), `"clean MVR"` (line 28), `"MVR review"` (line 44). Even as seed/mock data, criminal-history and MVR records are sensitive PII; committing realistic-looking entries sets a dangerous precedent if this file is ever populated with real applicant data (it backs a screening dashboard). Err-high on PII.
Fix: replace with obviously synthetic placeholders (e.g. `"Candidate A"`, generic notes); source real candidate/MVR/criminal data at runtime from an access-controlled API/DB, never the repo. Add a lint/CI guard against committing MVR/criminal strings.

### 🟡 Low

**L1 — Unused, deprecated dependency in attack surface**
`package.json:13` — `@google/generative-ai ^0.24.1` is declared but **not imported anywhere in `src/`**. It's also the deprecated SDK (superseded by `@google/genai`). Carrying unused deps widens the supply-chain surface for no benefit.
Fix: `npm uninstall @google/generative-ai` until it's actually wired in; adopt `@google/genai` when the Gemini integration lands.

### Clean (verified, no findings)
- **Secrets:** no hardcoded keys/tokens/credentials in `src/`. `.env.example` holds empty placeholders only; `.env`, `*.pem`, `.gcp/` are gitignored. ✅
- **Injection / risky patterns:** no `eval`/`exec`/`new Function`/`child_process`/`dangerouslySetInnerHTML`/raw SQL/`fetch`/`process.env` in source — app is currently static/presentational with no I/O or auth surface. ✅

*Note: this is the first security scan on record for this codebase — no prior findings in memory to dedupe against. The 3 findings above are persisted as signatures for next-run progress tracking.*

title: Version decision: Apprenticeship Placement v0.2.0 (MINOR)
content: Released v0.2.0 on 2026-05-27. Bump type MINOR (0.1.0 → 0.2.0). Rationale: pre-1.0 (0.x) project; per SemVer 0.x rules and this repo's stated CHANGELOG convention, BREAKING changes increment MINOR rather than MAJOR. The release content was the platform migration (Next.js 14→16, React 18→19, Tailwind 3→4, TypeScript 5→6, ESLint 9→10 — all breaking; plus Google Generative AI SDK 0.21→0.24.1), committed in 2fee916. Discovered the release was half-completed: CHANGELOG [0.2.0] entry existed but package.json was still 0.1.0 and no tag existed. Completed it: bumped package.json, wrote docs/releases/v0.2.0.md, created annotated tag v0.2.0. CONVENTION FOR NEXT TIME: this project keeps breaking=MINOR while under 0.x; the first 1.0.0 will be the threshold where breaking becomes MAJOR. No prior tags existed before v0.2.0 — v0.2.0 is the first release tag.
